sourceMovie.title: Gone Girl
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Gone Girl (2014) as a mystery theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The mystery and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Gone Girl clicked for you, A Walk Among the Tombstones (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Gone Girl?
faq.answer: Try A Walk Among the Tombstones, Kiss the Girls, A Fall from Grace—each shares DNA with Gone Girl while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Gone Girl?
faq.answer: Gone Girl (2014) is best described as Mystery, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Gone Girl worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
